name: Scoop
description: Command-line installer for Windows focused on open-source developer tools — installs to user directory, no admin.

seo.title: Scoop — CI/CD for Applications
seo.meta_description: Scoop: Command-line installer for Windows focused on open-source developer tools — installs to user directory, no admin.

when_to_use: Install developer CLI tools on Windows without admin
User-directory scoped package installs for CI agents
Automated Windows dev setup with scoop.sh manifests
Manage development tool versions with Scoop buckets

pros: Installs to user dir, no admin rights needed
Great for developer CLI tools on Windows
Buckets let teams curate their own package sets
cons: Less suited for GUI apps and enterprise rollouts
Some tools need manual PATH fiddling
review.verdict: The go-to for devs who want a Homebrew-like experience on Windows without admin prompts.
review.best_for: Windows developers installing CLI dev tools without admin or IT involvement.
review.not_for: IT departments doing enterprise-wide deployments — stick with Chocolatey or WinGet.

developer_experience.reason: Simple CLI, good Windows developer focus
